visual_checks: Check UI component boundaries: do form field borders have at least 3:1 contrast against their background?
Check button borders or backgrounds against the surrounding page background — at least 3:1?
Look at icons that convey information (checkmarks, warning icons, navigation arrows) — 3:1 against background?
Check focus indicators: do they have at least 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors?
Look at chart elements: do bars, lines, and segments have 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors?
Check toggle switches, checkboxes, and radio buttons for sufficient contrast
Examine custom-styled form controls (dropdowns, sliders, range inputs)
pass_conditions: UI components (buttons, form fields, toggles) have visual boundaries with at least 3:1 contrast ratio against adjacent colors
Graphical objects required to understand content (icons, chart elements) have at least 3:1 contrast ratio
Custom focus indicators have at least 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors
State changes in UI components (checked/unchecked, selected/unselected) have at least 3:1 contrast
Required parts of charts and graphs (bars, lines, pie slices) have at least 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors
fail_conditions: Form field borders with less than 3:1 contrast against the page background (e.g., light gray border on white)
Buttons with borders or backgrounds that do not contrast sufficiently with the page background
Icons conveying meaning (error, success, navigation) with less than 3:1 contrast
Chart bars or line graph lines with less than 3:1 contrast against their background
Custom checkboxes or radio buttons where the visual indicator has insufficient contrast
Focus indicators with less than 3:1 contrast against the background
na_conditions: Native HTML form controls styled with the browser default appearance (user agent styles are exempt)
UI components whose appearance is entirely controlled by the user agent and not modified by the author
Graphical objects that are purely decorative and not needed to understand the content
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Cannot measure exact contrast ratios for non-text elements from screenshots alone
Custom component styles are dynamically applied and not captured in the data
common_mistakes: Light gray (#ccc) input field borders on white (#fff) backgrounds — only 1.6:1 contrast
Flat-design buttons that blend into the background with no visible boundary
Chart segments in similar light pastel colors that do not contrast with each other
Custom toggle switches where the 'off' state has very low contrast
Icon buttons (hamburger menu, search icon) in light gray on white
Focus outlines in light blue on white backgrounds

examples.pass: Form input fields have a #767676 border on a #ffffff background (4.48:1 contrast), buttons have a solid #333 background, and all chart bars have at least 3:1 contrast against the white chart background
examples.fail: Text input fields have a #dddddd border on a #ffffff background (1.46:1 contrast) — the field boundaries are nearly invisible, especially for users with low vision
examples.partial: Buttons and primary form fields meet 3:1 contrast but secondary inputs (search bars, filter dropdowns) have very light borders that fail the 3:1 minimum

auditor_anti_patterns: FALSE POSITIVE (CRITICAL — invented contrast value): estimating a non-text contrast ratio from a screenshot when the captured `focus_contrast`, `pixel_contrast`, or `computed_styles` arrays already contain DETERMINISTIC measured ratios for that element. Use the captured value verbatim. If focus_contrast measured the focus indicator at 1.1 against the background, write 1.1:1 (and report failure). If it measured 4.0, write 4.0:1 (and report pass). Do not invent or estimate.

FALSE POSITIVE: flagging contrast/color of elements that are not visible to sighted users. Bootstrap '.visually-hidden' / '.sr-only' (and the older '.screen-reader-text', '.visuallyhidden') are CSS utility classes that clip the element to a 1px box and remove it from the visual layout while leaving it in the accessibility tree. The element is intentionally only readable by screen readers -- it is not rendered to the screen at all -- so visual rules like color contrast, font size, and luminance ratios DO NOT APPLY. Before flagging text or a control for contrast, check whether the element (or any ancestor) carries one of these utility classes or an equivalent clip:rect(0,0,0,0)/position:absolute/width:1px/height:1px pattern. If it does, do NOT report a contrast finding -- there is no rendered pixel to measure.
plain_meaning: The visual parts that let a user identify a UI component (boundaries, states) and the meaningful parts of informational graphics must have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1 against adjacent colors. This covers non-text visuals the same way 1.4.3 covers text. Inactive components, pure decoration, and logotypes are exempt.
scope_applies_to: Visible boundaries of form inputs, buttons, and other controls when the boundary is the primary identification
Icons that are the sole means of identifying a control
Selected, checked, expanded, or active state indicators on controls
Meaningful parts of charts, graphs, and infographics required to understand the data
Custom focus, hover, or selection styles when they are the only identification of the state
scope_does_not_apply_to: Elements removed from the accessibility tree (aria-hidden, display:none)
Visually-hidden / sr-only content (no rendered pixels to measure)
Purely decorative graphics that carry no information
Text and images of text (those are SC 1.4.3 / 1.4.6)
Controls identified by visible text label (the text identifies it; the border contrast is not required)
Focus indicator visibility as a standalone concern (that is SC 2.4.7; contrast of the indicator falls under 1.4.11 only when the indicator is the sole identifier of focused state)
borderline_calibration: Measured ratios of 3:1 or higher PASS.
A button with a visible text label and no visible border is NOT a failure; the label identifies the control.
Do NOT flag sr-only / visually-hidden elements.
Chart series distinguished only by low-contrast hues can fail both 1.4.1 (color alone) and 1.4.11 (non-text contrast). Choose the closer fit.
Borderline measurements within 0.1 of 3:1 should be reported with reduced severity.
